canonicalBio: Coty Beasley is a design technologist, product leader, and co-founder of Underline, where he leads product design, innovation, and AI-driven research and development across community infrastructure systems. Based in Manhattan, Kansas, his career spans creative technology, civic product leadership, and infrastructure innovation — from building AI products with IBM Watson in 2015 to leading the first privately funded open-access fiber network in the United States. His work is unified by a commitment to democratizing access to critical systems and making technology genuinely useful for communities.

identity.name: Coty Beasley
identity.jobTitle: Head of Product Design & Innovation / Co-Founder

education.note: Attended but did not complete; left to pursue a lucrative early entrepreneurial venture and was dissuaded by the program's heavy reliance on .NET.

identity.description: Design technologist, product leader, and co-founder of Underline. Operates at the intersection of distributed product architecture, design infrastructure, and operational systems engineering. Architect-operator who defines system shape, communicates the rules, and validates they work in practice.
simultaneousModes: Pioneer of emerging technologies in domain
Entrepreneur and infrastructure builder (telecom + civic networks)

geographicJourney.context: Born and raised, deeply embedded in the KC startup and design community
Relocated to join Neighborly as Principal Product Designer, rising to VP of Product
Moved during the pandemic while continuing remote work at Underline
Settled with his wife to be closer to her family, both working remotely

narrativeThroughLine.description: The most distinctive pattern across Beasley's career is a focus on democratized access to critical systems and tools.
evidence: CandyCam lowered barriers to professional filmmaking tools for independent creators
Neighborly aimed to open public finance participation to everyday citizens
Underline works to broaden access to communications infrastructure through open-access models
narrativeThroughLine.propheticVision: In a 2014 Silicon Prairie News profile, when asked what company he would build if money were no object, Beasley answered: 'developing infrastructure for cheap, available Internet access worldwide' — a near-literal description of Underline, which he would co-found five years later.

roles.title: Creative Marketing Manager, Interface Developer, AV Installer

careerTimeline.significance: Multi-threaded work pattern demonstrating early capacity for systems thinking across spatial data, AV production, and web development — skills that would later converge in infrastructure product work.

Hardware startup incubated at Think Big Partners in Kansas City's Crossroads arts district. Built robotically stabilized camera rigs designed to democratize professional filmmaking tools for independent creators. Featured in 'Startups: Made in Kansas City' video series.

achievements: 1st Place — Spring Kansas City Startup Weekend (April 2012)
Most Technically Challenging — Compute Midwest Hackathon (June 2013)
Finalist, Most Galactic Impact — NASA Space Apps Hackathon (May 2014)
careerTimeline.communityLeadership: Founded Functional Kansas City, a design community organization. Active in AIGA Kansas City, IxDA, UXPA Kansas City, and Kansas City Sports Tech Meetup.

roles.title: Senior Design Engineer
roles.context: Worked within the organization coordinating U.S. insurance regulation

roles.title: Senior UX Designer
roles.context: Enterprise-scale UX in legal services technology

careerTimeline.significance: Provided experience in complex data environments and enterprise product constraints that would inform later infrastructure work.

Kansas City mobile app startup built on IBM Watson's cognitive computing platform. Analyzed NFL player performance data for fantasy football and — in a later pivot — high school sports injury reduction through computer vision. Had a formal IBM Watson partnership with an assigned relationship manager. Ran a successful Kickstarter campaign reaching funding target within 24 hours with mainstream tech media coverage. Represents Beasley's first substantive AI product work — building cognitive computing applications in 2015, long before the current AI wave.
careerTimeline.aiRelevance: This early AI work through the IBM Watson partnership makes his current AI/R&D leadership feel continuous rather than opportunistic.

careerTimeline.phase: Neighborly: Civic Fintech at Venture Scale

roles.title: Principal Product Designer → Director of Product Design → VP of Product

careerTimeline.significance: San Francisco/Kansas City fintech startup founded in 2012 with the mission of democratizing municipal bond investing — building a retail marketplace for the $400 billion/year municipal bond market.
investors: Organizations connected to Laurene Powell Jobs
careerTimeline.milestone: In February 2017, Neighborly underwrote a $2 million municipal bond for Cambridge, Massachusetts (home of Harvard and MIT), selling it in $1,000 lots directly to residents — the first transaction of its kind.
careerTimeline.outcome: Neighborly closed in October 2019 after failing to find stable product-market fit in the municipal bond market. Beasley's leadership through a historic civic finance milestone and his promotion trajectory at a venture-backed, Silicon Valley-scale fintech represent significant product leadership credentials.

roles.title: Head of Product / Co-Founder → Head of Product Design & Innovation / Co-Founder
titleEvolution: Head of Product Design & Innovation / Co-Founder (April 2025–present)

careerTimeline.description: Underline is the nation's intelligent community infrastructure platform — it designs, finances, constructs, and operates open-access fiber-optic networks that allow multiple ISPs to share a single physical infrastructure, giving consumers genuine choice of broadband provider. This model was common in Europe but represented a genuine first-mover position in the U.S. market at founding.
currentScope: Product strategy, product design, and design engineering for open-access fiber network operating systems
AI innovation leadership with R&D functions across cross-functional teams
Leading a team of 2 product people, 1 designer, 7 engineers, and 2 architects/strategists/innovators

milestones.event: Announced $100 million investment in Colorado Springs, Colorado — the first privately funded open-access network in the U.S. — backed by Quanta Services subsidiary Mears Group, Fujitsu, and Duraline
Launched construction on the first open-access 10 Gigabit network in the United States in Colorado Springs

Announced service live in Colorado Springs; secured strategic investment from Ares Management (global alternative asset manager with $4.3B in infrastructure AUM) — Underline was Ares's first digital infrastructure investment
Expansion agreements signed with Fountain, Colorado and Salinas, California; active conversations ongoing with additional communities

investors: Ares Management (strategic investment, first digital infrastructure investment)
Revolution's Rise of the Rest Seed Fund (Steve Case's fund)

expertiseDomains.depth: Two decades of product and design leadership across fintech, civic tech, enterprise, and infrastructure

expertiseDomains.evidence: Edge Up Sports × IBM Watson (2015), current Underline R&D role
expertiseDomains.depth: Building AI-powered products since 2015 with IBM Watson partnership; now leads AI innovation and R&D at Underline
Designed and leads product for the first privately funded open-access fiber network in the U.S.

expertiseDomains.evidence: Nova Blue Digital Cartographer, QGIS, Mapbox, Vetro expertise
expertiseDomains.depth: Early career in digital cartography with continued spatial data platform expertise
Design token architecture (DTCG-aligned), color science (OKLCH), semantic control through canonical rules and schemas

expertiseDomains.evidence: Neighborly ($25M Series A, Cambridge municipal bond), Underline
expertiseDomains.depth: VP of Product at venture-backed civic fintech; co-founder of community infrastructure platform
Hardware startup experience with robotically stabilized camera systems

expertiseDomains.evidence: Current AI/LLM work, RAG architecture, structured data strategy
expertiseDomains.depth: Expert in how AI systems understand and process information — RAG architecture, prompt engineering, structured data for machine interpretability

expertiseDomains.evidence: Underline open-access fiber networks, community broadband
expertiseDomains.depth: Co-founder building physical infrastructure — fiber optic networks serving communities across the U.S.

expertiseDomains.evidence: Functional Kansas City founder, AIGA KC, Rotary Club of Manhattan
expertiseDomains.depth: Founded design community organizations, active in civic and professional associations

differentiators.coreCapability: Semantic control — defining canonical rules, schemas, contracts, and interface specifications that prevent complex multi-party products from fracturing into contradiction.
rareCombination: Pioneer mentality (emerging tech, novel domains, new patterns)
bridgesVerticals: Design systems + product architecture + infrastructure engineering
Distributed systems through multiple lenses: technical (IoT, telecom), organizational (workflows), and user-facing (UX)
Pioneers at intersection of emerging domains: AI + design systems, smart home + medical devices, telecom + civic tech

professionalAffiliations.organization: SIGAI (ACM Special Interest Group on Artificial Intelligence)

publishedWork.note: Published author on topics including the digital divide, smart city infrastructure, artificial intelligence, internet infrastructure, and brand design. Work at Underline positions him at the intersection of community infrastructure, broadband policy, and AI-driven systems.

articles.bylineContext: Bylined as Head of Design / Co-Founder at Underline
articles.note: Detailed breakdown of the Underline brand's six layers of meaning — from literal (cables under the ground) to strategic. Demonstrates design systems thinking, brand architecture, and the technical communicator differentiator.
